eyebrow.zh: 数字公民破解 · 一年级

chrome.sub: Make smart choices on the tablet. Solve four locks. Use the clues to help you decide.
chrome.briefP: Every day online you make choices. A Smart Chooser keeps a password private, is kind and stands up for others, asks a grown-up before tapping, and shares only safe things. Read the clues. Then open all four locks. Each lock shows you why the smart choice is the right choice.
chrome.footerText: Digital Citizenship Breakouts · Runs entirely in your browser — no logins, no data collected.
chrome.winP: You keep your password private. You are kind and an upstander. You ask a grown-up before you tap. You share only safe things. You are a Smart Chooser!

clues.title: Passwords stay private
clues.body: A password is your secret key. Keep it private. Do not tell friends. Only you and a helper grown-up should know it.

clues.title: Be kind and help
clues.body: An upstander helps when someone is treated badly. Do not laugh at a mean post. Be kind and tell a grown-up.

clues.title: Ask before you tap
clues.body: See a new button or a download? Do not tap yet. Ask a grown-up first. They tell you if it is safe.

clues.title: Share only safe things
clues.body: Some things are safe to share, like a drawing. Some are not, like your home. Think before you share.

clues.title: Stop and think
clues.body: Before you share or tap, stop and think. Ask: is this safe? is this kind? Then choose.

clues.title: Our class plant
clues.body: Our class grows a bean plant on the windowsill. It gets taller every week. This is fun, but it will not open a lock.

locks.title: Your Password
locks.q: A game asks you to type your password so a friend can see. What should you do?
options: Type it so your friend can play too
Keep it private and do not type it there
Change it to '1234' so it is easy to share
locks.reason: A password is a secret key that keeps your things safe. Sharing it lets others in, and '1234' is too easy to guess. Keeping it private is the smart choice.

locks.title: Be an Upstander

items.t: Be kind to someone getting mean posts
Tell a trusted grown-up about a mean post
Give a friend a nice compliment
Laugh at a mean post and share it
Join in and type mean words too

locks.reason: An upstander helps — be kind, tell a grown-up, give a compliment. Laughing at, sharing, or joining a mean post hurts someone, so we leave those unchecked.

locks.title: Before You Tap
locks.q: Before you tap a new button or a download, first ______ a grown-up.
locks.reason: A grown-up can tell you if a button or download is safe. Asking first stops you from tapping something that could be harmful.

locks.title: What Is OK to Share?
locks.q: You want to share something online. Tap the steps in the RIGHT order.
locks.reason: First THINK about what you want to share. Then ASK a grown-up if it is okay. Only then SHARE, and share only safe things.
